description_long: This is a sleek, futuristic airplane with a streamlined white fuselage and distinctive black and blue tail fins, branded with the 'Virgin' logo. The aircraft features a series of small, rounded windows along the side, suggesting a passenger configuration. Its wings are mounted mid-fuselage and sweep backward, with pronounced upward-angled winglets at the tips. The twin tail booms extend rearward, each supporting a vertical stabilizer with a dramatic, upward flare. The nose is sharply pointed, and the undercarriage includes visible landing gear. The overall design is reminiscent of a suborbital spaceplane, optimized for high-speed atmospheric and near-space travel.
description: A streamlined, white and black airplane with swept mid-mounted wings, twin upward-flared tail fins, and 'Virgin' branding. Features include a pointed nose, passenger windows, and visible landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, passenger windows, and both tail fins with 'Virgin' branding.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the pointed nose, cockpit windows, and the wide, swept wings with upward-angled tips.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the tail booms and vertical stabilizers.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the twin tail booms, upward-flared stabilizers, and the engine nozzle at the back.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with swept wings and pronounced upward-angled winglets at the tips.
attribute_engine_type: Single rear-mounted jet engine integrated between the tail booms.
attribute_tail_design: Twin vertical stabilizers mounted on extended tail booms, forming an H-tail configuration.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main units and a single nose gear, all appearing retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
